title: Pos
author: alisonntest-collab
body:
import sqlite3

# Database ချိတ်ဆက်ခြင်း
conn = sqlite3.connect('store_pos.db')
cursor = conn.cursor()

# Table တည်ဆောက်ခြင်း
cursor.execute('''CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS products 
                  (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT, price REAL, stock INTEGER)''')

# ပစ္စည်းအသစ် ထည့်ခြင်း function
def add_product(name, price, stock):
    cursor.execute("INSERT INTO products (name, price, stock) VALUES (?, ?, ?)", (name, price, stock))
    conn.commit()
    print(f"{name} ကို စာရင်းသွင်းပြီးပါပြီ။")

# အရောင်းမှတ်တမ်းတင်ခြင်း (Stock လခြင်း)
def make_sale(product_id, qty):
    cursor.execute("UPDATE products SET stock = stock - ? WHERE id = ?", (qty, product_id))
    conn.commit()
    print("အရောင်းအောင်မြင်ပါသည်။")

# စမ်းသပ်ကြည့်ရန်
# add_product("Coffee Mix", 500, 100)

title: Publish ":wear:watchface" APK and consume it in ":wear" module
author: bingranl
body:
Currently, the `:wear` module consumes the APK from the `:wear:watchface` module by directly accessing its build directory and creating manual task dependencies. This implementation breaks project isolation due to accessing another project and is brittle. Any change to the output location or task structure in `:wear:watchface` would require a corresponding manual update in the `:wear` module's build script.

See code block here https://github.com/android/androidify/blob/main/wear/build.gradle.kts#L94-L127C5

This should be refactored by publishing the built APK of `:wear:watch` and consuming it in the `:wear` module.

title: Enable dependency signature verification
author: liutikas
body: Enable dependency signature verification to make sure that used dependencies have not been tampered with. Follow https://developer.android.com/build/dependency-verification guide to do this.

title: Refactor common build logic into a set of Gradle plugin
author: liutikas
body: Refactor common build logic into a set of Gradle plugin. Right now build.gradle.kts files contain a lot of repetition. It would be more maintainable to have a small set of centralized plugins.

title: GitHub link in Play Store description is broken
author: symmetricalboy
body:
(Not exactly part of this repo, since it is regarding the publication on Google Play, but....)

In the Play Store listing when viewing on the Android app, the last sentence of the description: "Developers can explore the code on GitHub at https://github.com/android/androidify." needs a minor formatting fix (I would solve it with a space between the ending "y" & "." characters); clicking the link as it is sends the final period within the payload to the browser & shows the GH 404.

title: Project keep rules should not be needed
author: sgjesse
body: The current project keep rules in `app/proguard-rules.pro` are more broad than needed. As this is a Jetpack Compose app using common libraries, I would expect that most if not all rules can be removed and the app should still work.

title: Replace usage of Log.d etc with Timber, install Crashlytics Tree
author: riggaroo
body: We should use Timber instead of standard log, for automatic exception logging to Crashlytics when needed.

title: Make volume buttons take a photo
author: liutikas
body: It is super helpful to use volume buttons on the phone when taking a selfie. Google camera does that and it would be great if Androidify did the same

title: The app fails to run as expected
author: dailystudio
body:
I followed the steps outlined in the `README.md`:

1. Cloned the repository
2. Opened it in Android Studio
3. Set up a Firebase project and downloaded the `google-services.json` file
4. Enabled the Vertex AI API

At this point, the app compiled successfully. However, when I tried to run it, I encountered the following error:
`com.google.firebase.ai.type.ServerException: Firebase AI Logic is missing a configured Gemini Developer API key.`

Upon inspecting `FirebaseAiDataSource.kt` at line 253, I found the following code:

```kotlin
val model = Firebase.ai(backend = GenerativeBackend.googleAI()).generativeModel(
    modelName = remoteConfigDataSource.getImageGenerationEditsModelName(),
    generationConfig = generationConfig {
        responseModalities = listOf(
            ResponseModality.TEXT,
            ResponseModality.IMAGE,
        )
    },
)
```

Since this uses the Google Developer API, I updated it to use the `vertexAI()` backend instead. Unfortunately, the error persisted. As a next step, I enabled the Gemini Developer API in the Firebase console:

5. Enabled the Gemini Developer API in the Firebase project

After doing so, I attempted to run the app again, but it failed with a different error message:
`com.google.firebase.ai.type.ServerException: models/androidify is not found for API version v1main, or is not supported for generateContent. Call ListModels to see the list of available models and their supported methods.`

I suspect this is due to the fact that the `models/androidify` model is not available in my Vertex AI model registry.

Could you help me determine what additional steps are required to run the app successfully?

title: Make app multi-language support
author: erfansn
body:
I aim to enhance the app by making it multilingual, with Persian being the first language I would like to implement. 
May I be permitted to do that?

title: Please someone build and release the apk.
author: overandoutnerd

title: Migrate ResultsScreen when logic to use Nav3 instead
author: riggaroo
body: The Results screen is currently handling a conditional `when` for switching between `Loading` and `CustomizeAndExport`, this means we have to handle back ourselves, leaving predictive back not working for these use cases.
